content: For autonomous computer-using agents that directly control mouse, keyboard, and screen interactions, we can integrate a graded memory and reflection module that only triggers recursive self-improvement after the agent completes high-stakes, multi-step computer tasks, rather than reflecting on every low-level action. This module will categorize interaction histories by task outcome, then rewrite the agent’s low-level action prior distribution to avoid repeated misclicks, wrong input sequences, and dead-end workflow branches that reinforcement learning alone misses.
